interpretations.interpretation: The post-9/11 US sacred political order demonstrates a hybrid pattern: high sacred-political-order strength achieved through sacred-enemy escalation rather than constitutional-containment. The AUMF's unlimited force authorization and Bush's globally totalized binary ('with us or with the terrorists') temporarily maximized the sacred political order's strength by sacralizing national identity through absolute external threat. This distinguishes post-9/11 from the Vietnam aftermath (which produced constitutional-containment via the WPR) and from the gold high-pathology cases (where sacred-enemy escalation targeted internal populations). The 'forever war' trajectory shows the instability of strength-through-sacred-enemy — the enemy definition expanded indefinitely, producing institutional fragmentation rather than consolidation.

confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Chain thin; passages establish the founding pattern but trajectory scoring requires expansion. Hybrid case complexity adds uncertainty.

interpretations.mechanismSummary: 9/11 shock was channeled into a globally totalized sacred-enemy binary through unlimited congressional force authorization and presidential framing, temporarily maximizing sacred-political-order strength at the cost of institutional constraint and long-term legitimacy.

claims.claim: The post-9/11 United States sacred political order responded to the attacks through sacred-enemy escalation: the AUMF granted the executive unlimited force authorization against a broadly defined enemy, while Bush's congressional address constructed a globally totalized binary — 'with us or with the terrorists' — that sacralized the US sacred political order through opposition to an absolute external threat, foreclosing the constitutional-containment alternative.

confidence.label: moderate-high
confidence.rationale: Both passages are public-law and public-address texts verified against official sources. The AUMF's open-ended authorization and Bush's binary framing are the canonical founding documents of the post-9/11 order. Confidence capped pending chain expansion including Patriot Act, drone campaign, and torture program documentation.

passages.locator: Authorization for Use of Military Force, Public Law 107-40, § 2(a), 18 September 2001, 115 Stat. 224; text verified at https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/PLAW-107publ40/html/PLAW-107publ40.htm
passages.text: That the President is authorized to use all necessary and appropriate force against those nations, organizations, or persons he determines planned, authorized, committed, or aided the terrorist attacks that occurred on September 11, 2001, or harbored such organizations or persons, in order to prevent any future acts of international terrorism against the United States by such nations, organizations or persons.

source.notes: Public law. Full text at govinfo.gov.

passages.locator: George W. Bush, Address to a Joint Session of Congress and the American People, 20 September 2001; text verified at https://www.whatsoproudlywehail.org/curriculum/the-american-calendar/address-to-a-joint-session-of-congress-following-the-911-attacks/
passages.text: Every nation, in every region, now has a decision to make: Either you are with us, or you are with the terrorists. From this day forward, any nation that continues to harbor or support terrorism will be regarded by the United States as a hostile regime.

source.notes: Public domain presidential address. Verified at whatsoproudlywehail.org.

confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Score of 4 reflects high but not maximum sacred-political-order strength: the immediate post-9/11 order achieved strong public cohesion and institutional mobilization, but the unlimited enemy definition and absence of constitutional containment produced instability. Not maximum because the order's strength was fear-driven and began declining within years of the founding moment.

scores.reviewNote: Score value 4/5 defensible: sacred-enemy-escalation mechanism (AUMF's unlimited scope, Bush address framing) supports elevated but not maximum sacred-political-order-strength — the constitutional framework survived and eventually provided restraint mechanisms. Confidence 0.60 reflects chain-requires-expansion and the broadening-enemy-definition uncertainty. Hold at score-review until chain expansion to ≥4 passages.
